title: Payment Infrastructure for South Asian Creators: The Minimum Viable Setup That Actually Works — The Full Guide
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Selling ebooks, courses, and digital products in Pakistan, Nigeria, Ghana, or Kenya is fraught with restrictions and limitations. Your sales are blocked by PayPal, Stripe, or Payhip's geographic restrictions, leaving you with no choice but to abandon your business or lose money due to high transaction fees. Creating a payment infrastructure for the digital creator stuck in South Asia requires a specific, blockchain-based solution that works with 300+ coins, no KYC, and instant automated delivery.
What You Can Actually Get Done Today

You can get started with the minimum viable payment setup today by signing up for a NOWPayments account, which does not require a buyer account or KYC verification. This setup will allow you to receive international payments in your crypto wallet, giving you the freedom to sell your digital products to clients globally without being restricted by traditional payment processors. With NOWPayments, you can accept over 300 different cryptocurrencies, eliminating the need for multiple payment gateways or currency conversions.

How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. Once you receive USDT in your wallet, you convert to local currency through P2P exchanges — Binance P2P, Paxful, or local platforms specific to your country. The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
The Full Stack Bundle is the minimum viable payment setup that works for digital creators in South Asia.
